Social Perception of Body Morphology in Sports Students
Pilot study
Master of Science (MSc) — Engineering and Ergonomics of Physical Activity
Faculty of Sports and Health Sciences (UFR STAPS)
University of Montpellier
Author: Delphine Hund (Master 1 IEAP Digimove)
Supervisors: Ludovic Marin, Mathilde Parisi, Stéphane Raffard, Rachel F. Rodgers
Course: Python, R and Git for Data Analysis
Academic Year: 2025–2026
Date of Defense: Tuesday 17 March 2026
GitHub Repository: https://github.com/DelphineHun-student/Hund.Delphine-Project

02.1_processing.Rmd — Statistical Report (Final validation)

Experiment summary

This pilot study aims to validate the perceived morphology of dynamic avatars. Participants viewed 4 animated avatars (randomized order) and rated each avatar on 6 adjective-based dimensions: athletic, average, overweight, obese, thin, skinny.

The mapping between ratings and avatars is reconstructed using: - VS03_01 … VS03_04: the avatar identifier presented at each position (slot) - PM01_01 … PM72_01: 72 ratings corresponding to 12 blocks of 6 adjectives (fixed order within each block)

Hypotheses

For each avatar, ratings will be higher on the adjective matching its intended morphology than on the non-matching adjectives (congruent > incongruent).

Final validation

The present analyses are based on the last version of the pilot study questionnaire and stimulus set, including the following modifications:

  • Updated female athletic avatar (more muscle).
  • Updated female skinny avatr (thinner).
  • Updated male thin avatar (thinner).

1 Initiate

1.1 Packages

project_root <- normalizePath("..", winslash = "/")
print(project_root)
## [1] "/Users/delphinehund/Desktop/Stage/Pilot_Study-SocBodPer/V2/code"
print(file.exists(file.path(project_root, "renv", "activate.R")))
## [1] TRUE

1.2 Set directory

PATH <- dirname(getwd()) 
DAT_PATH = file.path(PATH, "DAT")
CLEAN_PATH = file.path(DAT_PATH, "clean")
RES_PATH = file.path(PATH, "RES")
TABLES_DIR <- file.path(RES_PATH, "tables_revalidation")

1.3 Read data

file_rdata <- file.path(
  project_root, "DAT", "clean", "pre_manip_ratings_new.rdata"
)
load(file_rdata)

2 Data preparation

2.1 Data integrity check

We verify that each participant contributes the expected number of ratings (12 avatars × 6 adjectives = 72).

ratings_long %>%
  count(CASE) %>%
  summarise(min_n = min(n), max_n = max(n))
## # A tibble: 1 × 2
##   min_n max_n
##   <int> <int>
## 1    24    24

2.2 Variables types

We explicitly define variable types to ensure correct statistical modeling.

ratings_long <- ratings_long %>%
  mutate(
    CASE = as.factor(CASE), # Participant identifier
    avatar = as.character(avatar), # Avatar label
    adjectif = as.factor(adjectif), # Within-subject factor
    note = as.numeric(note), # Rating value
    DE03x01 = as.numeric(DE03x01), # Age
    DE08 = as.numeric(DE08) # Gender code (1 = Female ; 2 = Male ; 3 = Other)
  )
## Warning: There were 3 warnings in `mutate()`.
## The first warning was:
## ℹ In argument: `note = as.numeric(note)`.
## Caused by warning:
## ! NAs introduced by coercion
## ℹ Run `dplyr::last_dplyr_warnings()` to see the 2 remaining warnings.

2.3 Exclusion

Participants reporting significant eating disorder or body dysmorphic symptoms (DE10 = 2 or 3) were excluded from further analyses.

excluded_participants <- ratings_long %>%
  filter(DE10 %in% c(2, 3)) %>%
  distinct(CASE)

n_total <- ratings_long %>%
  distinct(CASE) %>%
  nrow()

n_excluded <- nrow(excluded_participants)
n_included <- n_total - n_excluded

ratings_clean <- ratings_long %>%
  filter(!(CASE %in% excluded_participants$CASE))

exclusion_summary <- tibble(
  Category = c("Total participants", 
               "Excluded (DE10 = 2 or 3)", 
               "Included in analyses"),
  N = c(n_total, n_excluded, n_included)
)
exclusion_summary
## # A tibble: 3 × 2
##   Category                     N
##   <chr>                    <int>
## 1 Total participants          26
## 2 Excluded (DE10 = 2 or 3)     6
## 3 Included in analyses        20

3 Descriptive statistics

3.1 Demographics: sample description

Age (mean ± SD) and gender distribution for the final included sample.

demographics <- ratings_clean %>%
  distinct(CASE, DE03x01, DE08)

demographics_summary <- demographics %>%    
  summarise(
    N_total = n(),
    Mean_age = round(mean(DE03x01, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    SD_age = round(sd(DE03x01, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    N_female = sum(DE08 == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    N_male   = sum(DE08 == 2, na.rm = TRUE),
    N_other  = sum(DE08 == 3,na.rm = TRUE)
  )

demographics_summary
## # A tibble: 1 × 6
##   N_total Mean_age SD_age N_female N_male N_other
##     <int>    <dbl>  <dbl>    <int>  <int>   <int>
## 1      20     36.7   16.9        9     10       0

3.2 Ratings by avatar × adjective

We report mean and standard deviation of ratings for each avatar across the six adjective dimensions.

desc_avatar_adj <- ratings_clean %>%
  group_by(avatar, adjectif) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean = mean(note),
    SD = round(sd(note, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    N = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(avatar, adjectif)

desc_avatar_adj

3.3 Export

wb_desc <- createWorkbook()

addWorksheet(wb_desc, "exclusion_summary")
writeData(wb_desc, "exclusion_summary", exclusion_summary)

addWorksheet(wb_desc, "demographics_summary")
writeData(wb_desc, "demographics_summary", demographics_summary)

addWorksheet(wb_desc, "ratings_avatar_adj")
writeData(wb_desc, "ratings_avatar_adj", desc_avatar_adj)

saveWorkbook(
  wb_desc,
  file = file.path(TABLES_DIR, "descriptive_statistics.xlsx"),
  overwrite = TRUE
)

save_table_png <- function(df, filename){
  gt_table <- gt(df)
  gtsave(
    gt_table,
    filename = filename
  )
}

save_table_png(
  exclusion_summary,
  file.path(TABLES_DIR, "exclusion_summary.png")
)

)

ratings_clean %>%
  group_by(avatar) %>%
  summarise(
    n_participants = n_distinct(CASE),
    n_adjectifs = n_distinct(adjectif),
    n_rows = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
## # A tibble: 8 × 4

## 2 F_SKINNY_W_D.mp4             19           6    114

4 Manip-check

4.1 Congruence code

We extract each avatar’s intended morphology from its filename (e.g., _ATH_, _AVG_) and define whether each rating is congruent (adjective matches intended morphology) or incongruent.

avatars_valides <- c(
  "F_ATH_W_D.mp4",
  "F_SKINNY_W_D.mp4",
  "F_THIN_W_D.mp4",
  "M_THIN_W_D.mp4"
)

ratings_clean <- ratings_clean %>%
  filter(avatar %in% avatars_valides)
morpho_levels <- c("athletic","average","overweight","obese","thin","skinny")

ratings_clean <- ratings_clean %>%
  mutate(
    morpho_target = case_when(
      str_detect(avatar, "_ATH_")    ~ "athletic",
      str_detect(avatar, "_AVG_")    ~ "average",
      str_detect(avatar, "_OBESE_")  ~ "obese",
      str_detect(avatar, "_THIN_")   ~ "thin",
      str_detect(avatar, "_SKINNY_") ~ "skinny",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    morpho_target = factor(morpho_target, levels = morpho_levels),
    congruence = ifelse(adjectif == morpho_target, "congruent", "incongruent"),
    congruence = factor(congruence, levels = c("incongruent", "congruent"))
  )
# Check
table_morpho <- table(ratings_clean$morpho_target, useNA = "ifany")
table_congruence <- table(ratings_clean$congruence, useNA = "ifany")

table_morpho
## 
##   athletic    average overweight      obese       thin     skinny 
##        114          0          0          0        228        114
table_congruence
## 
## incongruent   congruent 
##         380          76

4.2 RM-ANOVA

For each avatar, we first test whether there is a global effect of adjective using a repeated-measures ANOVA (within-subject factor: adjective).

adjectifs <- levels(ratings_clean$adjectif)

# RM-ANOVA per avatar (global effect of adjective)
run_rm_anova <- function(data_av){ # data_av = data of ONE avatar
  data_av <- data_av %>% mutate(CASE = as.factor(CASE))

  aov_mod <- aov(note ~ adjectif + Error(CASE/adjectif), data = data_av)
  aov_sum <- summary(aov_mod)

  # Extract the adjective effect (within-subject)
  aov_table <- tryCatch({
    tab <- as.data.frame(aov_sum[["Error: CASE:adjectif"]][[1]]) %>%
      tibble::rownames_to_column("term") %>%
      filter(term == "adjectif") %>%
      transmute(
        df1 = Df,
        df2 = aov_sum[["Error: CASE:adjectif"]][[1]]["Residuals","Df"],
        F = `F value`,
        p_value = `Pr(>F)`
      )
    tab
  }, error = function(e){
    tibble(df1 = NA, df2 = NA, F = NA, p_value = NA)
  })
  
  aov_table
}

4.3 Planned contrasts

We then conduct planned contrasts comparing the target adjective (i.e., the adjective corresponding to the avatar’s intended morphology) against each of the five non-target adjectives.

All post-hoc comparisons are corrected for multiple testing using the Holm method.

run_planned_contrasts <- function(data_av, adjectives = adjectifs) {
  target <- unique(data_av$morpho_target)
  stopifnot(length(target) == 1)
  
  lm_mod <- lm(note ~ CASE + adjectif, data = data_av)
  emm <- emmeans(lm_mod, ~ adjectif)

  others <- adjectifs[adjectifs != target]

  contr_list <- setNames(
    lapply(others, function(adj){
      v <- rep(0, length(adjectifs)); names(v) <- adjectifs
      v[target] <- 1
      v[adj] <- -1
      v
    }),
    paste0(target, " - ", others)
  )

  ph <- contrast(emm, contr_list) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    transmute(
      compared_to = str_replace(contrast, paste0("^", target, " - "), ""),
      estimate = estimate,
      SE = SE,
      df = df,
      t = t.ratio,
      p_value = p.value
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      p_adj = p.adjust(p_value, method = "holm"),
      significant = p_adj < .05
    ) 
  ph
}

out <- ratings_clean %>%
  group_by(avatar) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    data_av <- .x
    target <- unique(data_av$morpho_target)

    tibble(
      morpho_target = target,
      anova = list(run_rm_anova(data_av)),
      posthoc = list(run_planned_contrasts(data_av))
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup()

anova_by_avatar <- out %>%
  tidyr::unnest(anova) %>%
  select(avatar, morpho_target, df1, df2, F, p_value)

posthoc_by_avatar <- out %>%
  tidyr::unnest(posthoc) %>%
  select(avatar, morpho_target, compared_to, estimate, t, p_value, p_adj, significant)

anova_by_avatar
## # A tibble: 0 × 6
## # ℹ 6 variables: avatar <chr>, morpho_target <fct>, df1 <dbl>, df2 <dbl>,
## #   F <dbl>, p_value <dbl>
posthoc_by_avatar
## # A tibble: 20 × 8
##    avatar           morpho_target compared_to estimate      t  p_value    p_adj
##    <chr>            <fct>         <chr>          <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>
##  1 F_ATH_W_D.mp4    athletic      average        33.2   5.19  1.28e- 6 1.28e- 6
##  2 F_ATH_W_D.mp4    athletic      overweight     62.8   9.83  6.56e-16 1.97e-15
##  3 F_ATH_W_D.mp4    athletic      obese          65.6  10.3   8.16e-17 3.26e-16
##  4 F_ATH_W_D.mp4    athletic      thin           51.8   8.10  2.56e-12 5.12e-12
##  5 F_ATH_W_D.mp4    athletic      skinny         73.8  11.5   1.87e-19 9.36e-19
##  6 F_SKINNY_W_D.mp4 skinny        athletic       41.8   5.08  2.03e- 6 6.08e- 6
##  7 F_SKINNY_W_D.mp4 skinny        average        38.9   4.73  8.31e- 6 1.66e- 5
##  8 F_SKINNY_W_D.mp4 skinny        overweight     67.6   8.21  1.54e-12 6.14e-12
##  9 F_SKINNY_W_D.mp4 skinny        obese          71.4   8.67  1.66e-13 8.28e-13
## 10 F_SKINNY_W_D.mp4 skinny        thin           -2.26 -0.275 7.84e- 1 7.84e- 1
## 11 F_THIN_W_D.mp4   thin          athletic       16.4   2.14  3.48e- 2 6.97e- 2
## 12 F_THIN_W_D.mp4   thin          average         7.47  0.975 3.32e- 1 3.32e- 1
## 13 F_THIN_W_D.mp4   thin          overweight     72.1   9.40  5.01e-15 2.50e-14
## 14 F_THIN_W_D.mp4   thin          obese          71.3   9.31  7.94e-15 3.18e-14
## 15 F_THIN_W_D.mp4   thin          skinny         40.1   5.23  1.08e- 6 3.24e- 6
## 16 M_THIN_W_D.mp4   thin          athletic       46.4   5.61  2.23e- 7 6.68e- 7
## 17 M_THIN_W_D.mp4   thin          average        16.7   2.02  4.66e- 2 4.66e- 2
## 18 M_THIN_W_D.mp4   thin          overweight     66.8   8.08  2.82e-12 1.41e-11
## 19 M_THIN_W_D.mp4   thin          obese          62.7   7.59  2.86e-11 1.15e-10
## 20 M_THIN_W_D.mp4   thin          skinny         34.4   4.16  7.35e- 5 1.47e- 4
## # ℹ 1 more variable: significant <lgl>

4.4 Validation summary

An avatar is considered validated if all planned contrasts (target adjective vs each non-target adjective) are statistically significant after Holm correction.

avatar_validation <- posthoc_by_avatar %>%
  group_by(avatar, morpho_target) %>%
  summarise(
    n_contrasts = n(),
    n_significant = sum(significant),
    all_contrasts_significant = all(significant),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(all_contrasts_significant), avatar)

avatar_validation
## # A tibble: 4 × 5
##   avatar          morpho_target n_contrasts n_significant all_contrasts_signif…¹
##   <chr>           <fct>               <int>         <int> <lgl>                 
## 1 F_ATH_W_D.mp4   athletic                5             5 TRUE                  
## 2 M_THIN_W_D.mp4  thin                    5             5 TRUE                  
## 3 F_SKINNY_W_D.m… skinny                  5             4 FALSE                 
## 4 F_THIN_W_D.mp4  thin                    5             3 FALSE                 
## # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​all_contrasts_significant
# Export
save_table_png(
  avatar_validation,
  file.path(TABLES_DIR, "avatar_validation.png")
)
## file:////var/folders/s7/l40675nx1_b8x31mxcqljfqr0000gn/T//Rtmp4N2cYF/file2cad6603ba88.html screenshot completed

5 Plots and export

For each avatar, we plot mean ratings (± SEM) for the six adjectives. The intended adjective (congruent) is highlighted.
Plots are exported as one PNG file per avatar in RES/plots_avatar/.

# Mean + SEM per avatar × adjective
plot_data <- ratings_clean %>%
  group_by(avatar, adjectif, morpho_target) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_note = mean(note, na.rm = TRUE),
    sem = sd(note, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(note))),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Significance labels from post-hoc results
sig_labels <- posthoc_by_avatar %>%
  mutate(label = ifelse(significant, "*", "ns")) %>%
  left_join(
    plot_data,
    by = c("avatar", "morpho_target", "compared_to" = "adjectif")
  ) %>%
  mutate(y_pos = mean_note + sem + 2)

# Plot function (one avatar)
plot_one_avatar <- function(av_id){

  pd_av <- plot_data %>% filter(avatar == av_id)
  sg_av <- sig_labels %>% filter(avatar == av_id)

  # Cas particulier : pour F_THIN_W_D.mp4, afficher seulement THIN et SKINNY
  if (av_id == "F_THIN_W_D.mp4") {
    pd_av <- pd_av %>%
      filter(adjectif %in% c("thin", "skinny"))

    sg_av <- sg_av %>%
      filter(compared_to %in% c("skinny"))
  }

  ggplot(pd_av, aes(x = adjectif, y = mean_note)) +
    geom_col(aes(fill = adjectif == morpho_target), width = 0.7) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_note - sem, ymax = mean_note + sem), width = 0.2) +
    geom_text(
      data = sg_av,
      aes(x = compared_to, y = y_pos, label = label),
      size = 4
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c("grey", "steelblue"),
      labels = c("Incongruent", "Congruent"),
      name = ""
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Avatar:", av_id),
      subtitle = "Planned contrasts: target adjective vs each non-target",
      x = "Adjective",
      y = "Mean rating"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "top"
    )
}

# Export one PNG file per avatar
avatar_list <- sort(unique(plot_data$avatar))
walk(avatar_list, ~ print(plot_one_avatar(.x)))

PLOTS_DIR <- file.path(RES_PATH, "plots_avatar_new")
if (!dir.exists(PLOTS_DIR)) dir.create(PLOTS_DIR, recursive = TRUE)

walk(avatar_list, function(av){
  p <- plot_one_avatar(av)
  safe_name <- str_replace_all(av, "[^A-Za-z0-9_-]", "_")
  out_png <- file.path(PLOTS_DIR, paste0("plot_", safe_name, ".png"))

  ggsave(
    filename = out_png,
    plot = p,
    width = 10, height = 6, units = "in",
    dpi = 300, bg = "white"
  )
})
